channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Sage Cloud Hosting Providers: How to Choose the Right One
item.description: Finding the right Sage cloud hosting provider is about more than moving your accounting software to the cloud. The ideal provider should deliver secure access, reliable performance, and expert support while ensuring your Sage environment remains available whenever your team needs it. Whether you...
item.dc:creator: DavisMiller29

item.title: Using the Small Window for Incoming Teams Calls
item.description: A recent improvement is the introduction of a small window for incoming Teams calls. It doesn’t sound like a great step forward, but switching to the small window is a surprisingly useful step. The sad thing is that many users won’t know that the option to switch exists because it’s buried in Teams...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: .NET 8 and .NET 9 will reach End of Support on November 10, 2026
item.description: .NET 8 and .NET 9 will both reach end of support on November 10, 2026. Learn what this means and how to upgrade to .NET 10 (LTS). The post .NET 8 and .NET 9 will reach End of Support on November 10, 2026 appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Rahul Bhandari (MSFT)

item.title: SkiaSharp 4.0 is here: announcing the first stable release
item.description: SkiaSharp 4.148.0 is the first stable release of SkiaSharp v4. Learn what is new, why you should be excited, and join the SkiaSharp live event on June 30. The post SkiaSharp 4.0 is here: announcing the first stable release appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Leibowitz

item.title: Announcing General Availability of Client-Side Data Integrity Protections in Azure Blob Storage
item.description: We are excited to announce the General Availability of client-side data integrity in Azure Blob Storage. Blob Storage has long supported integrity validation, starting with MD5 as part of the original HTTP standards and adding CRC64-NVME support in 2019. Today, CRC64-NVME is now integrated into the...
item.dc:creator: wmarkley

item.title: Highlights from Git 2.55
item.description: The open source Git project just released Git 2.55. Here is GitHub’s look at some of the most interesting features and changes introduced since last time. The post Highlights from Git 2.55 appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Packaging and Package Identity for .NET apps with WinApp CLI on Windows
item.description: Use the WinApp CLI to add identity to and package .NET desktop applications, enabling deeper integration to Windows and easier distribution. The post Packaging and Package Identity for .NET apps with WinApp CLI on Windows appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Teutsch

item.title: Claude Opus 4.8 (fast mode) is now in preview for GitHub Copilot
item.description: Claude Opus 4.8 (fast mode) is now rolling out in preview on GitHub Copilot. Fast mode delivers significantly faster output token speeds while maintaining the same intelligence as Claude Opus… The post Claude Opus 4.8 (fast mode) is now in preview for GitHub Copilot appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Chromium extension uses AI‑related branding to redirect browser search
item.description: A malicious Chromium-based extension that spoofs the AI-powered answer engine Perplexity AI redirects browser search traffic using MV3 APIs and intermediary infrastructure. The post Chromium extension uses AI‑related branding to redirect browser search appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Inside the Advisory Database and what happens when vulnerability volume breaks records
item.description: The GitHub Advisory Database is processing more vulnerability reports than ever before. Here's what's driving the surge, how we're responding, and how the community can help. The post Inside the Advisory Database and what happens when vulnerability volume breaks records appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Restrict issue creation to collaborators only
item.description: Repository admins can now restrict issue creation to collaborators with write access. This gives you more control over who can open issues and helps reduce unwanted issue creation, while keeping… The post Restrict issue creation to collaborators only appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Your readiness playbook: adoption helper, costs, APIs, and the checklist
item.description: Co-authored with Lizet Pena, Caroline Mutua, Alvin Kua and Marco Sudahl The transition is not a project—it is a sequence of small, ordered decisions. Here is the readiness tool that scores your environment in minutes, the cost story you can take to finance, the API strategy that future-proofs your...
item.dc:creator: Mohit_Kumar1

item.title: Secure Azure and Fabric Event Flows Across Workspaces with Outbound Access Protection (Preview)
item.description: Lock down cross-workspace event flows in Fabric: new Outbound Access Protection lets you control which workspaces can consume Azure and Fabric events—blocking unauthorized access by default until you explicitly allow the Real-Time Events connector.
item.dc:creator: GEGUIRGU

item.title: WSL container is now available for public preview
item.description: At Microsoft Build 2026, we introduced WSL containers, bringing Linux container development directly into Windows through the Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL). Containers have become a foundational part of modern development – from cloud-native applications and AI workloads to testing and...
item.dc:creator: Craig Loewen

item.title: Introducing EWSAllowedAppIDs: Preparing for the Final Phase of EWS Retirement
item.description: Exchange Web Services (EWS) retirement in Exchange Online is entering its final phase. Over the last several years, Microsoft has worked with product teams, independent software vendors (ISVs), and customers across the ecosystem to migrate workloads to Microsoft Graph and other modern APIs. Many of...
item.dc:creator: The_Exchange_Team

item.title: Automating your Visual Studio extension builds with GitHub Actions
item.description: If you’re building and maintaining Visual Studio extensions, you’ve probably ended up with some sort of build and publishing workflow – whether it’s manual, scripted, or stitched together over time. This post is for extension authors who want a simple, repeatable way to build, version, and publish...
item.dc:creator: Mads Kristensen

item.title: Remote Event Receivers are retiring: move to SharePoint webhooks before July 1, 2027
item.description: Remote Event Receivers in SharePoint Online are retiring. Starting July 1, 2027, all remote event receivers will stop firing events, including those registered using Microsoft Entra applications. The post Remote Event Receivers are retiring: move to SharePoint webhooks before July 1, 2027 appeared...
item.dc:creator: SharePoint team

item.title: External Teams federation broken after Dunloe Dataplane Service provisioning failure
item.description: Repost without tenantIDSince June 26, all external Teams chat/federation is broken for our organization. Messages to all external users fail with "Send failed".In our Entra audit log we found the root cause: on June 26 at 05:57 UTC, Microsoft Azure AD Internal - Jit Provisioning failed to provision...
item.dc:creator: RichardTilman1

item.title: Spring AI 2.0 is GA: Vector Search, Memory, and Agents on Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: The wait is over. Spring AI 2.0 is generally available, and Azure Cosmos DB is right there with it. With this release, Spring AI graduates into a mature, production-ready framework for building AI applications in Java, and Azure Cosmos DB ships dedicated, vendor-maintained integrations that plug...
item.dc:creator: Theo van Kraay

item.title: Intune Platform Scripts never target devices (0 targeted devices) despite healthy Intune environment
item.description: Hi everyone,I'm hoping someone has seen this before because I've exhausted most of the obvious troubleshooting.EnvironmentMicrosoft 365 Business PremiumWindows 11 ProMicrosoft IntuneMicrosoft Entra ID Joined devicesIntune Management Extension (IME) installed and healthyThe IssuePlatform Scripts...
item.dc:creator: HRZook

item.title: External Teams federation broken after Dunloe Dataplane Service provisioning failure
item.description: Since June 26, all external Teams chat/federation is broken for our organization. Messages to all external users fail with "Send failed".In our Entra audit log we found the root cause: on June 26 at 05:57 UTC, Microsoft Azure AD Internal - Jit Provisioning failed to provision "Dunloe Dataplane...
item.dc:creator: RichardTilman1

item.title: Microsoft 365 E7 and Agent 365 Are Now Generally Available: The Future of AI-Powered
item.description: The workplace is entering a new era where artificial intelligence is no longer just a helpful tool — it is becoming an active partner in how people work, collaborate, and make decisions. Microsoft has taken another major step in this direction with the general availability of Microsoft 365 E7 and...
item.dc:creator: JohnNaguib

item.title: Need to Restore PST Files to Office 365 Mailboxes - What's the Best Approach
item.description: Hey everyone,I have a task coming up where I need to restore several PST files back into Office 365 mailboxes. Haven't done this before at this scale and honestly not sure where to begin.I've looked at Microsoft's native import service through Purview but I have a few concerns:Some of the PST files...
item.dc:creator: KastroMoff

item.title: MCP for Beginners: Why Every AI Engineer and Developer Should Learn the Model Context Protocol
item.description: If you have spent any time building with large language models in the last year, you have hit the same wall everyone hits: your model is brilliant at reasoning but blind to the real world. It cannot read your database, call your internal API, search your documents, or trigger a deployment unless...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: More resilient Spark jobs with Efficient Scaledown (Preview)
item.description: Running large-scale data engineering workloads in Apache Spark often means dealing with expensive shuffle operations. Shuffle is one of the most resource-intensive operations that Spark runs on most, if not all, jobs. When executors holding shuffle data are removed during scale-down, Spark must...
item.dc:creator: Santhosh_Ravin1

item.title: Track total merges by adoption phase in enterprise and organization reports
item.description: Building on the AI adoption phase cohorts added to the Copilot usage metrics API, organization and enterprise reports now report the total number of pull requests merged by each adoption… The post Track total merges by adoption phase in enterprise and organization reports appeared first on The...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: ISO Download error
item.description: Im in iran and i got an error download windows insider build iso
item.dc:creator: Artafp

item.title: Read-only Actions cache for untrusted triggers
item.description: GitHub Actions now issues read-only cache tokens to the default branch for workflow events that can be triggered without write permissions to the repository. This applies least privilege to the… The post Read-only Actions cache for untrusted triggers appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: The next frontier in endpoint security: Securing local AI agents with Microsoft Defender
item.description: AI agents are now doing real work on the endpoint — reading files, running commands, browsing the web, and acting on behalf of the users they run under. That same power is also what makes them dangerous: agents act on whatever content they take in, and much of it comes from outside the user's...
item.dc:creator: Eitan_Shteinberg

item.title: GitHub and UNDP team up to advance development priorities in Ghana with open source
item.description: GitHub joined the United Nations Development Programme in Ghana to explore how open source governance can support one of West Africa's most ambitious digital reform efforts. The post GitHub and UNDP team up to advance development priorities in Ghana with open source appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Azure Developer CLI (azd) – May and June 2026
item.description: A combined May and June recap. A new azd tool command group, the cross-platform azd exec runner, safer multi-layer provisioning, an interactive Ctrl+C cancel prompt, Go support for Azure Functions, self-contained extension bundles, per-tenant subscription filters, and a long list of correctness...
item.dc:creator: Kristen Womack

item.title: Windows 11 license transfer problem on a Virtual Machine
item.description: Hi everyone,I'm stuck with a Windows 11 Pro activation issue after a VM crash and could use some advice.Context: > Years ago, I got a Windows 10 Pro digital license through the Windows Insider Program and used it on bare metal. Fast forward to about two years ago, I successfully migrated that...
item.dc:creator: Alfos

item.title: MAI-Code-1-Flash for Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise
item.description: MAI-Code-1-Flash, Microsoft AI’s in-house coding model, is now generally available for GitHub Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise, building on its recent expansion across Copilot surfaces. Purpose-built for coding and optimized… The post MAI-Code-1-Flash for Copilot Business and Copilot...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: How to determine which Resource Mailboxes are being actively used
item.description: Today we wanted to take a few minutes to discuss a topic that has come up several times. Consider the scenario where your organization has created Resource mailboxes, and you want to know which ones are actually being used. Seems like a fair request. This would include Room and Equipment mailboxes...
item.dc:creator: The_Exchange_Team

item.title: Transitioning as a Hubber
item.description: How GitHub's culture and benefits helped me be the best version of myself. The post Transitioning as a Hubber appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: GitHub Desktop 3.6: Worktrees and deeper Copilot integration
item.description: GitHub Desktop 3.6 brings more of your day-to-day Git flow into one place with GitHub Copilot now powering commit authoring and merge conflict resolution, plus new Git worktree support. The… The post GitHub Desktop 3.6: Worktrees and deeper Copilot integration appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: What the DragonForce Attack Against Teams Is All About
item.description: Recent reports from security companies and others about an attack by the DragonForce ransomware group against a U.S. services company highlighted the use of Teams. It’s true that Teams was involved in this very artful attack, but the most critical point is that the network was compromised well...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Passing Context Between Agents in Multi-Agent A2A Systems
item.description: How we solved the challenge of passing conversational context between independently deployed agents using the A2A protocol's embedded context pattern, keeping domain agents stateless and secure. The post Passing Context Between Agents in Multi-Agent A2A Systems appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Olha Konstantinova, Scott Grosenick

item.title: Persistent File Explorer Crashes When Navigating Specific Network Locations on Windows PC
item.description: Whenever I try to navigate to certain network locations on my Windows PC, which interrupts my ability to access shared files smoothly. The crashes happen consistently when accessing specific network folders, causing the application to close unexpectedly and making it difficult to work efficiently...
item.dc:creator: Kujawaa

item.title: Persistent File Explorer Crashes When Navigating Specific Network Locations on Windows PC
item.description: I am experiencing persistent crashes of File Explorer whenever I try to navigate to certain network locations on my Windows PC, which interrupts my ability to access shared files smoothly. The crashes happen consistently when accessing specific network folders, causing the application to close...
item.dc:creator: Pompi

item.title: Infrequent Blue Screen Errors Triggered by Firmware Conflicts
item.description: I am experiencing infrequent blue screen errors on my computer, which seem to be triggered by firmware conflicts. These crashes happen sporadically during normal use, and they often point to issues related to hardware firmware or driver compatibility, causing system instability and unexpected...
item.dc:creator: Harodman

item.title: Unusual Power Drain When System Is Idle
item.description: I have noticed an unusual power drain on my system even when it is idle, with the battery percentage decreasing significantly without any active tasks or background processes running. Despite ensuring that all unnecessary programs are closed and performing regular system maintenance, the power...
item.dc:creator: Edgerson

item.title: Is there a difference between Insider's Balanced and Best Performance power modes?
item.description: For new Windows 11 laptops equipped with Modern Standby, what are the actual differences in performance test results between the Balanced and Best Performance modes in Settings?This is what I want to figure out. What are the differences between the preview version and other versions?
item.dc:creator: ZacharyRodriguez

item.title: Insider: The Photos feature in File Explorer cannot load images and keeps showing Loading...
item.description: After updating to the latest Insider Preview build, the Photos feature in the left-hand navigation pane of File Explorer fails to load images properly. The interface continuously displays a spinning Loading... icon, does not show any image thumbnails, and cannot be restored to normal by...
item.dc:creator: NicholasLewis

item.title: After upgrading to the Insider Preview, an issue has arisen with the Performance tab in Task Manager
item.description: After updating to the latest Insider Preview build, the detailed information—such as GPU usage and video memory usage—that was previously displayed on the Performance tab in Task Manager suddenly disappeared. Now, it either displays No GPU detected or the section appears blank, even though the...
item.dc:creator: RileyMurphy

item.title: What IT teams need to know about Linux Secure Boot certificates expiring in 2026
item.description: If your organization does not use UEFI Secure Boot on Linux systems, this transition does not affect your boot path. You can stop reading now. If you do use Secure Boot, here is what you need to know. The Microsoft Corporation UEFI CA (Certificate Authority) 2011 expires on June 27, 2026 (June 26...
item.dc:creator: bexelbie

item.title: Use built-in Fabric data protection to get your data AI-ready
item.description: Every organization wants to put AI to work on its data. Copilot, agents, and generative AI promise faster insight, but they raise a hard question for security teams: If AI can reach all our data, what stops it from surfacing the wrong data to the wrong person? The answer is to make your data...
item.dc:creator: antonfr

item.title: Using Bulk Copy API for faster ingestion in Fabric Data Warehouse (Preview)
item.description: The BCP API provides a client-side ingestion path for scenarios where data is produced within application code and needs to be written directly to warehouse tables without intermediate staging. It can deliver 5–10× higher ingestion throughput from the client compared to standard T-SQL INSERT...
item.dc:creator: jovanpop-msft

item.title: Evaluating performance and efficiency of the GitHub Copilot agentic harness across models and tasks
item.description: Explore how the GitHub Copilot agentic harness delivers strong results across multiple benchmarks and leading token efficiency, while maintaining flexibility to choose among more than 20 models. The post Evaluating performance and efficiency of the GitHub Copilot agentic harness across models and...
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Photo ZIP campaign targeting hospitality industry delivers Node.js implant for persistent access
item.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence identified an active multi-stage intrusion campaign targeting hospitality organizations in Europe and Asia. The campaign uses photo-themed ZIP archives and fake image shortcut files to deliver a persistent Node.js implant and evade detection. The post Photo ZIP...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team and Parth Jomadkar
